death.cause: Killed in action / died in army service, War of 1812

military_service.notes: Per EH #1494 and Hart Genealogy of 1875: 'soldier of the War of 1812, died in the army' at Sandusky, Ohio in August 1813 aged seventeen. Specific unit and circumstances not yet researched. The Battle of Fort Stephenson at Sandusky was fought August 1-2, 1813 — Norman's August death is consistent with that engagement, though could also reflect typical disease casualties at frontier garrisons.

cross_connections.display_label: Drowned in Lake Erie at Sandusky, 1836
cross_connections.notes: Both Hooker descendants died at Sandusky, Ohio, twenty-three years apart. Norman Hart (a great-grandson of Nathaniel Hart through the Marietta line) died there August 1813 as a soldier of the War of 1812, aged seventeen. Sherman Pierpont (descended through the James Pierpont III line) drowned in Lake Erie at Sandusky on May 7, 1836, aged fifty-two — his body washed ashore three weeks later. Sandusky was an early Connecticut-frontier convergence point and both men ended their lives at it through different paths.

narrative_blocks.header: Eldest son killed at Sandusky, summer 1813
narrative_blocks.body: Born August 29, 1795 at the family's Marietta home in Ohio, eldest of Selah Hart and Sarah Walter's ten children. He enlisted as a soldier in the War of 1812 and died at Sandusky, Ohio in August 1813, aged seventeen. The August timing aligns with the defense of Fort Stephenson on August 1-2, 1813 under Major George Croghan — Sandusky's most notable engagement of the war.

sources.short: Andrews, Genealogical History of Deacon Stephen Hart (1875)

sources.short: Andrews, Genealogical History of Deacon Stephen Hart (1875)

death.cause: Killed by fall of tree (or limb of tree per Hart Genealogy)

naming_inspiration.description: Named for his uncle Gen. Selah Hart of Kensington — the same uncle who later adopted his orphaned brother Cyprian (H00562) in 1773 and raised him at the General Hart homestead.

narrative_blocks.header: Settled at Marietta on the Ohio frontier
narrative_blocks.body: Fifth son of Nathaniel Hart No. 2, named for his uncle General Selah Hart of Kensington who had adopted his brother Cyprian. Migrated from Connecticut to the Ohio frontier — among the early settlers at Marietta, Washington County, in the Ohio Company's territory. EH #561 records his Ohio descendants at numbers 1494-1503.

narrative_blocks.header: Killed by a falling tree in Ohio, 1836
narrative_blocks.body: He died in Ohio in 1836, killed by a falling tree. One son Norman had been killed earlier at Sandusky in 1813 during the War of 1812. EH numbers ten Ohio-born descendants in the next generation.

narrative_blocks.header: Fourteen years a magistrate of excellent judgment
narrative_blocks.body: He served as a magistrate at Marion, Morgan County, Ohio for about fourteen years, and was generally accounted of good judgment and local influence. Both he and his wife Sarah were active church members from early life. The Hart Genealogy of 1875 records that the place was greatly missed and mourned over at his death, so great was the loss.

sources.short: Andrews, Genealogical History of Deacon Stephen Hart (1875)

bio.extra_names: EH #561 records maiden name as 'Walter'; the Hart Genealogy of 1875 records it as 'Waters'. Both forms attested.

crossConnections.display_label: Drowned in Lake Erie at Sandusky, 1836
